theme_notes: Touchstones: US-Iran Strait of Hormuz strikes continuing (ceasefire effectively over, oil sanctions reinstated); 2026 FIFA World Cup knockouts (England beats Mexico in 'game of the tournament', USA red card controversy, 48-team field); Wimbledon (Osaka shocks top-ranked Sabalenka, Serena withdraws from doubles); Ukraine drone campaign takes out 25 ships in the Sea of Azov; Congo Ebola outbreak near 600 dead with frontline workers quitting over unpaid wages; Trump disbands the US Election Assistance Commission.
settled: Turns out the ceasefire wasn't completely over. Your balance has better timing than Trump.
Osaka took down the world's top player today. You two only took down a bill.
England-Mexico was the game of the tournament. Your balance is the post-match nap.
youOwe: Naomi Osaka upset the world number one in less time than this debt has festered.
The USA got a red card at the World Cup. Your tab is the yellow card they missed.
Smaller than the CENTCOM strike tally on Day One. Just as impossible to ignore.
Congo's Ebola frontline workers haven't been paid since May. You're in similar standing.
A World Cup nosebleed seat — except you're the one getting the nosebleed.
The Trump voter ID bill is stuck in Congress. Your payment is stuck in your imagination.
The Tour de France peloton has covered more ground this week than your excuses have moved.
Ukraine drone-struck 25 ships in four days. Your tab has the same burn rate.
The Strait of Hormuz is technically open for shipping. Your wallet is not.
A World Cup quarterfinal seat — and you're the red card the stadium missed.
Centre Court at Wimbledon charges less for strawberries. You're getting served anyway.
Putin refuses to negotiate. You've found a kindred spirit.
Trump disbanded the entire Election Assistance Commission for less institutional failure.
Your debt just entered its Wimbledon phase: highly seeded, won't be knocked out easily.
Iran's oil sanctions were reversed, then reinstated in 20 days. Your tab outlasted both.
You are now a Strait of Hormuz tanker: all traffic blocked, on fire, causing an incident.
The World Cup expanded to 48 teams. Your excuses have expanded proportionally.
Russia and Ukraine are closer to peace than you are to a payment. Let that land.
theyOwe: Like Trump's faith in electoral oversight: technically on record, functionally gone.
Less than a World Cup souvenir program and equally already forgotten.
Every Tour de France stage moves faster than your partner's Venmo.
Wimbledon's grass has been cut tighter than your partner's wallet.
Your partner's funds are frozen tighter than Iranian oil exports. Sanctions pending.
Ukraine knocked out 25 Russian ships this week. Your partner is harder to reach.
A Ukraine attack drone costs about this. The thought has crossed your mind.
England-Mexico was the game of the tournament. This debt is the hangover.
Ebola frontline workers quit over this amount in unpaid wages. Your partner agrees it's nothing.
The Tour de France has 21 stages. Your partner hasn't completed stage one.
Iran and the US managed a 48-hour pause. Your partner can't manage a payment reminder.
Serena withdrew from Wimbledon doubles due to injury. Your partner has the same energy.
Your partner owes more than Congo's Ebola workers are owed in back wages. Remarkable.
Iran blinked after two days of airstrikes. Choose your escalation posture.
A World Cup round-of-sixteen trip. Your partner is playing away. As usual.
This has become a Strait of Hormuz situation. Sanctions are on the table.
The World Cup final hasn't been played yet. This debt predates it. Both will be historic.
Putin is more forthcoming about a peace deal than your partner is about payment.
